description: Scriptable Plugin System - Process-based plugin execution model where plugins are scripts (JS/TS) that run as separate OS processes, communicating with MRunner via JSON stdin/stdout

userStories.title: Add Rust types for scriptable plugins
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core Rust types for the plugin system so that discovery, registration, and execution have a shared type foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/launcher/src/plugins.rs module
Define PluginTier enum: Json, Scriptable (with serde Serialize/Deserialize)
Define CommandMode enum: List, Detail, Action (with serde)
Define RegisteredCommand struct: id (String), title, description, icon, mode (CommandMode), keywords (Vec<String>), script_path (PathBuf)
Define RegisteredPlugin struct: plugin_id, plugin_name, plugin_icon, runtime, commands (Vec<RegisteredCommand>), tier (PluginTier)
Add mod plugins to lib.rs
cargo check -p mrunner passes
userStories.notes: Types defined in design doc section 7. Use #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] on all types. serde rename_all = camelCase for JSON interop with frontend.

userStories.title: Implement plugin directory scanner
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that scans ~/.config/mrunner/plugins/ and discovers both Tier 1 JSON files and Tier 2 scriptable plugin directories.
acceptanceCriteria: Add discover_plugins() function in plugins.rs that scans the plugins directory
Tier 1: detect *.json files directly in plugins/ (existing behavior, just collect paths)
Tier 2: detect */plugin.json directories, parse plugin.json manifest
For Tier 2: scan src/commands/*/config.json within each plugin dir, parse each config.json
Build Vec<RegisteredPlugin> from discovered Tier 2 plugins with command IDs as 'plugin-id:command-name'
Handle missing/invalid files gracefully with error logging (don't crash on bad plugins)
cargo check -p mrunner passes
userStories.notes: Plugins dir: ~/.config/mrunner/plugins/ (use dirs::config_dir or tauri path API). plugin.json fields: id, name, version, description, author, icon, runtime. config.json fields: title, description, icon, mode, keywords. Command ID format: '{plugin_id}:{command_dir_name}'. Script path: {plugin_dir}/src/commands/{command_name}/command.ts.

userStories.title: Add discover_plugins Tauri command
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a Tauri IPC command to get the list of discovered plugins so the renderer can display them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create #[tauri::command] async fn discover_plugins() that calls the scanner from US-002
Return Vec<RegisteredPlugin> serialized as JSON to the frontend
Register the command in the invoke_handler in lib.rs
cargo check -p mrunner passes
userStories.notes: Register in apps/launcher/src/lib.rs invoke_handler array. Tauri commands registered there are available via invoke() in the renderer. Follow the pattern of existing commands in lib.rs.

userStories.title: Create @mrunner/plugin SDK package with types and Command class
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I need an npm package with the abstract Command class and TypeScript types so I can build plugins with type safety.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/plugin/ directory with package.json (name: @mrunner/plugin, private: false)
Add tsconfig.json extending the monorepo base config
Create src/index.ts exporting: Command abstract class, CommandContext interface, ListItem interface, Action type union, ListResult/DetailResult/ActionResult/CommandResult types, Accessory type
Command class has abstract run(context: CommandContext): Promise<CommandResult> and optional onItemSelect(itemId: string, context: CommandContext): Promise<CommandResult>
Action type union includes: url, open, copy, shell, notification, push variants as defined in the design doc
Add packages/plugin to pnpm-workspace.yaml if not already covered by glob
pnpm install succeeds and package is recognized in workspace
userStories.notes: Design doc section 2 has exact type definitions. CommandContext has query (string), preferences (Record<string, unknown>), environment ({ locale, theme, platform, homeDir }). Keep it minimal - no runtime dependencies. Build with tsup or tsc.

userStories.title: Add SDK runner CLI entrypoint
userStories.description: As a plugin system, I need a runner script that handles stdin/stdout plumbing so plugin authors never touch process I/O directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/plugin/src/runner.ts as a CLI entrypoint
Runner reads JSON from stdin (CommandContext)
Runner dynamically imports the command module path from argv[1]
Runner instantiates the exported default class (must extend Command)
Runner calls run(context) and writes JSON result to stdout
Runner writes errors to stderr and exits with code 1 on failure
Add bin entry in package.json pointing to the compiled runner
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Invocation: node packages/plugin/dist/runner.js /path/to/command.ts. The runner is what Rust spawns. Use process.stdin for reading, process.stdout.write for output. Handle the case where the imported module doesn't export a Command subclass.

userStories.title: Add JSON schemas for plugin.json and command config.json
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I need JSON schemas so I get IntelliSense in VS Code when editing plugin.json and config.json files.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/plugin/schemas/plugin.schema.json with properties: id, name, version, description, author, icon, runtime (enum: node, deno, bun, python, bash)
Create packages/plugin/schemas/command.schema.json with properties: title, description, icon, mode (enum: list, detail, action), keywords (array of strings)
Both schemas have $schema draft-07 reference and required fields
Icon field has enum values matching MRunner's predefined icon set
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Referenced via $schema in plugin files: node_modules/@mrunner/plugin/schemas/plugin.schema.json. Check existing icon names from the codebase (Lucide icons used in command palette). mode enum: list, detail, action.

userStories.title: Implement plugin command process spawner in Rust
userStories.description: As the system, I need to spawn plugin commands as child processes with stdin/stdout JSON communication and timeout enforcement.
acceptanceCriteria: Add run_plugin_command() function in plugins.rs
Function takes command_id (String) and context (JSON value) as parameters
Resolve the plugin's runtime (node/deno/bun) and script path from RegisteredPlugin data
Spawn child process: {runtime} {runner_path} {script_path}
Write context as JSON to child's stdin, then close stdin
Read stdout as JSON result with 10-second timeout
Capture stderr for error reporting
Return Result with parsed JSON on success, or error with stderr content on failure
Kill process on timeout and return timeout error
cargo check -p mrunner passes
userStories.notes: Use tokio::process::Command for async spawning. Runner path: find @mrunner/plugin/dist/runner.js relative to the plugin's node_modules. For timeout, use tokio::time::timeout. Non-zero exit code should be treated as an error. The runner path may need to be resolved from the plugin directory's node_modules.

userStories.title: Add run_plugin_command Tauri command
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a Tauri IPC command to execute a scriptable plugin command and get the result.
acceptanceCriteria: Create #[tauri::command] async fn run_plugin_command(command_id: String, context: serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String>
Command looks up the RegisteredPlugin/RegisteredCommand by command_id
Calls the process spawner from US-007
Returns the JSON result on success or error message on failure
Register the command in the invoke_handler in lib.rs
cargo check -p mrunner passes
userStories.notes: Plugin state (discovered plugins) needs to be accessible from the Tauri command. Use tauri::State<T> with a Mutex<Vec<RegisteredPlugin>> or similar pattern. command_id format: 'plugin-id:command-name'.

userStories.title: Extend use-plugins hook for Tier 2 plugins
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need the use-plugins hook to load both Tier 1 JSON plugins and Tier 2 scriptable plugins from the new discover_plugins command.
acceptanceCriteria: Update apps/renderer/src/hooks/use-plugins.ts to call invoke('discover_plugins') on mount
Add TypeScript types matching the Rust RegisteredPlugin/RegisteredCommand structs
Merge Tier 2 plugins into the existing plugin/command list alongside Tier 1
Tier 2 commands get a 'scriptable' flag or tier field to distinguish them in the UI
Loading and error states handled for the discovery call
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Existing use-plugins.ts handles Tier 1 JSON plugins. The hook should unify both tiers into a single commands list for the palette. Check the current hook implementation for the existing pattern.

userStories.title: Show scriptable plugin commands in CommandPalette
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see scriptable plugin commands in the command palette, grouped by plugin name, so I can discover and launch them.
acceptanceCriteria: Tier 2 plugin commands appear in the CommandPalette list
Commands are grouped/labeled by their parent plugin name
Each command shows its title, description, and icon
Selecting a list-mode command transitions to a sub-view for that command's results
Selecting an action-mode command triggers immediate execution via invoke('run_plugin_command')
Add i18n keys for any new user-facing text (en.json and pt-BR.json)
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Check how existing commands render in the palette to match the pattern. list mode should show a loading state while the command runs. action mode should show a notification on completion. Keywords from config.json should be searchable.

userStories.title: Implement list mode execution with dynamic results
userStories.description: As a user, I want list-mode plugin commands to show their results in the palette and re-execute when I change my search query.
acceptanceCriteria: When a list-mode command is active, invoke('run_plugin_command') with current query
Display returned ListItem[] in the palette (title, subtitle, icon, accessories)
Re-execute the command with debounce (300ms) when the search input changes
Show loading indicator during execution
Show error state if command fails or times out
Selecting a list item triggers its first action (or shows action menu if multiple)
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Use a debounced effect for re-execution on query change. The CommandContext.query field carries the current search text. ListItem has actions[] array - the primary action fires on Enter, secondary actions could show in a submenu.

userStories.title: Implement detail mode and action mode rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want detail-mode commands to show markdown content and action-mode commands to execute immediately with a notification.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail mode: render the returned markdown string in a panel (reuse existing Streamdown/markdown renderer)
Detail mode: show action buttons if the result includes actions[]
Action mode: execute immediately when command is selected, no sub-view
Action mode: show OS notification on success (title from command config)
Action mode: show error notification on failure
Add i18n keys for loading/error/success states (en.json and pt-BR.json)
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Check for an existing markdown renderer in the codebase (Streamdown is mentioned in the design doc). Action mode is fire-and-forget from the user's perspective. Use Tauri notification plugin or existing notification pattern.

userStories.title: Implement plugin action type handlers
userStories.description: As a user, I want all plugin action types (url, open, copy, shell, notification, push) to work when I interact with plugin results.
acceptanceCriteria: url action: opens URL in default browser via Tauri shell plugin
open action: opens file/folder in system default via Tauri shell plugin
copy action: copies content string to clipboard
shell action: executes command through existing is_command_allowed() whitelist check
notification action: shows OS notification with title and message
push action: navigates to another plugin command by command ID
All actions dispatched from a shared executeAction(action) utility function
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Most of these handlers likely already exist for Tier 1 plugins (url, open, copy, shell). Check existing action execution code and reuse. The 'push' action is new - it should set the active command in the palette to the target command ID.

userStories.title: Create Settings Plugins tab with installed plugins list
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Plugins tab in Settings where I can see my installed plugins and enable/disable them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/renderer/src/components/settings/plugins-tab.tsx
List all installed plugins with name, version, author, and icon
Each plugin has an enable/disable toggle that persists to preferences.json
Expandable section per plugin showing its commands (title, description, mode)
Add 'Open Plugins Folder' button that opens ~/.config/mrunner/plugins/ in file manager
Wire the tab into the existing settings sheet/dialog
Add i18n keys for all labels (en.json and pt-BR.json)
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Plugin enabled/disabled state stored in preferences.json under plugins key. Follow the existing settings tab pattern (check other tabs in the settings directory). Use @mrunner/ui components. The 'Open Plugins Folder' button uses Tauri shell plugin's open() with the directory path.

userStories.title: Add git-based plugin installation from URL
userStories.description: As a user, I want to install plugins from a Git URL so I can add community plugins to my MRunner setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Install from Git URL' input field in the Plugins settings tab
Create Rust Tauri command install_plugin(git_url: String) that: git clones to plugins dir, validates plugin.json, runs npm install if package.json exists
Show installation progress/status in the UI
On success: re-discover plugins and refresh the list
On failure: show error message and clean up the cloned directory
Add confirmation dialog before installation showing plugin name, author, and source
Register install_plugin in invoke_handler
Add i18n keys for install UI (en.json and pt-BR.json)
cargo check -p mrunner passes
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Use std::process::Command to run git clone and npm install. Clone target: ~/.config/mrunner/plugins/{plugin-id}/. The plugin-id comes from the cloned plugin.json. Validate plugin.json schema before accepting. Security: show user what they're installing before proceeding.

userStories.title: Add plugin registry browsing and update checking
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse available plugins from the official registry and check for updates to my installed plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Browse Plugins' section in Settings Plugins tab that fetches plugins/registry.json from the MRunner repo raw URL
Display registry plugins with name, description, author, tags, and verified badge
Show 'Install' button for plugins not yet installed, 'Installed' badge for ones already present
Add 'Check for Updates' button that runs git pull on each installed Tier 2 plugin directory
Show update results (updated/up-to-date/error per plugin)
If updates found: re-run npm install and re-discover plugins
Cache registry locally, refresh every 24h or on manual refresh
Add i18n keys for browse/update UI (en.json and pt-BR.json)
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: Create plugins/registry.json in the MRunner repo root with the format from design doc section 4. Registry URL: raw GitHub URL of the main repo's plugins/registry.json. For updates, use Rust Tauri command that runs git -C <plugin-dir> pull. Cache registry in ~/.config/mrunner/plugin-registry-cache.json with timestamp.
